interest: The interest of frontline teachers and their unions, who bear the concrete labour cost of any reform (retraining time, higher workload, class-size management during transition).
value: Pedagogical realism and fair treatment of the workforce in tension with administrative/technocratic elegance and speed of reform.
fear: That reform will again be announced and imposed on an already-exhausted profession as an unfunded expectation, with retraining and transition costs silently downloaded onto teachers.
affected: Students experiencing class-size and continuity effects during transition
The treasury/funder responsible for retraining budgets
assumption: That an audit designed without an explicit teacher-implementation-cost dimension and binding union consultation will produce reforms that are uncosted and infeasible in practice.
empirical_uncertainty: The general fact that unfunded reforms burden teachers is well-established, but whether this specific audit's TOR would omit implementation costing is contested and depends on decisions not yet made.
